**Hackman & Oldham (1976), "Motivation through the Design of Work"**
The Job Characteristics Model identifies skill variety, task identity, task significance, autonomy, and feedback as core drivers of motivation and satisfaction. Notably, "interesting work" is only one of several pillars — autonomy and impact matter comparably.

**Kahneman & Deaton (2010), "High income improves evaluation of life but not emotional well-being"**

**Killingsworth (2021), "Experienced well-being rises with income, even above $75,000/year"**
Challenges Kahneman & Deaton: well-being keeps rising with income, though the slope is modest. Relevant because it weakens the "money doesn't matter past X" intuition your friends may be implicitly relying on.

**Judge et al. (2010), "The relationship between pay and job satisfaction: A meta-analysis"**
Finds a surprisingly weak correlation (~.15) between pay level and job satisfaction. Supports the view that salary differences are overweighted in career decisions.

**Bottom line:** Your friends are directionally supported but overstating it.
